titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 561

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=832 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.440395+00 | translation moa=833 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.450249+00

p.cl: A . dei gratia rex scottorum . Omnibus per regnum suum scottis et anglis  salutem .

Sciatis quod
ego dono deo et Sancto Cuthberto domino meo et Monachis eius omnia que habebant
tam in terris quam in aquis die qua frater meus rex Eadgarus uiuus et mortuus fuit .
ita ... cum omnibus consuetudinibus sicut Monachi predicti illo eodem
die melius et quietius habuerunt illam eandem terram . Et nominatim illam terram quę iacet inter Horeuoredane et Cnapedane sicut breue fratris mei Eadgari eis
testatur .
Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all throughout his kingdom, Gaelic and English, greeting.

I present as a gift to God, and to Saint Cuthbert my lord, and to his monks, everything which they had, as much in lands as in waters, on the day in which my brother, King Edgar, was alive and dead, so ... with all customs, just as the aforesaid monks best and most quitly had that same land on that same day; specifically the land which lies between Horndean and Nabdean, just as the brieve of my brother Edgar bears witness.
